Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Interface IProofDependencies

public interface IProofDependencies
Interface encapsulating information that can be used to decide if a proof is reusable with another sequent. A proof is reusable for another sequent if:
  • It has no dependencies OR
  • The goal is null or is identical to the goal of the sequent and
  • All used hypotheses are contained in the hypotheses of the sequent and
  • All used free identifiers (with identical types) are contained in the type environment of the sequent and
  • No introduced free identifiers (ignoring their types) are present in the type environment of the sequent and
  • Used reasoners have no version conflict
  • There are no context dependent reasoners or all rules that use context dependent reasoners are reusable
Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Predicate
    getGoal()
    Returns the goal predicate of the proof, or null in the case where any goal will do.
    Set<String>
    getIntroducedFreeIdents()
    Returns the names of the free identifiers introduced in a proof.
    ISealedTypeEnvironment
    getUsedFreeIdents()
    Returns the type environment corresponding to the used free identifiers in a proof.
    Set<Predicate>
    getUsedHypotheses()
    Returns the hypotheses used in a proof.
    Set<IReasonerDesc>
    getUsedReasoners()
    Returns descriptors of all reasoners used in the proof.
    boolean
    hasDeps()
    Returns if the proof does indeed have dependencies.
    boolean
    isContextDependent()
    Returns whether the proof is context dependent, i.e uses context dependent reasoners.

  • Method Details

    • hasDeps

      boolean hasDeps()
      Returns if the proof does indeed have dependencies. The rest of the methods in this interface are only valid if hasDeps() returns true.
      Returns:
      false iff the proof can be reused for any sequent.

    • getGoal

      Predicate getGoal()
      Returns the goal predicate of the proof, or null in the case where any goal will do. In case the proof has no dependencies the result is undefined.
      Returns:
      the goal predicate of the proof.

    • getUsedHypotheses

      Set<Predicate> getUsedHypotheses()
      Returns the hypotheses used in a proof. In case the proof has no dependencies the result is undefined.
      Returns:
      the hypotheses used in a proof.

    • getUsedFreeIdents

      ISealedTypeEnvironment getUsedFreeIdents()
      Returns the type environment corresponding to the used free identifiers in a proof. In case the proof has no dependencies the result is undefined.
      Returns:
      the type environment corresponding to the used free identifiers in a proof.
      Since:
      3.0

    • getIntroducedFreeIdents

      Set<String> getIntroducedFreeIdents()
      Returns the names of the free identifiers introduced in a proof. These identifiers must remain fresh for the proof to be reused. In case the proof has no dependencies the result is undefined.
      Returns:
      the type environment corresponding to the free identifiers introduced in a proof.

    • getUsedReasoners

      Set<IReasonerDesc> getUsedReasoners()
      Returns descriptors of all reasoners used in the proof. These descriptors must not have version conflicts for the proof to be reused. In case the proof has no dependencies the result is undefined.
      Returns:
      a set of reasoner descriptors
      Since:
      2.2

    • isContextDependent

      boolean isContextDependent()
      Returns whether the proof is context dependent, i.e uses context dependent reasoners.
      Returns:
      true if the proof is context dependent, false otherwise
      Since:
      3.0
